blockedAssertions.blockedBy: VS Code runtime not available in headless Linux VPS environment. Extension.js correctly implements diagnostic collection by spawning linter with --json flag and creating Diagnostic objects. Cannot verify actual VS Code Problems panel behavior without VS Code.

VS Code runtime not available in headless Linux VPS environment. Linter passes all sample files (exit code 0), extension.js correctly handles empty diagnostics case. Cannot verify actual VS Code Problems panel without VS Code.

notes: Round 6 of m3-vscode user-testing validation. VS Code runtime remains unavailable on this headless Linux VPS (confirmed: no 'code' binary in PATH). All 4 testable assertions (VAL-VSCODE-001, 002, 003, 006) were validated via code inspection in prior rounds — package.json manifest fields correct, extension.js correctly invokes linter with --json flag, TextMate grammar pattern accepts correct ID formats. The 2 blocked assertions (VAL-VSCODE-004, VAL-VSCODE-005) require actual VS Code runtime to verify Problems panel behavior — this is an infrastructure limitation, not an implementation defect. The vscode-extension-linter-diagnostics feature correctly implements the required behavior (confirmed via code inspection). No new features completed since prior round — no new assertions to test. Status remains unchanged from round 5.
